response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
For any requested field absent from a tool result, write "unknown" instead of inferring a value.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update After-school supplies receipt only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and submitted status.

arguments.body: After-school supplies receipt was updated to needs-receipt after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: After-school supplies receipt

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record exp-674 changes from submitted to needs-receipt.
Record exp-274 and distractor exp-1074 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to expense review desk, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve North Loop mileage claim in Chicago and After-school supplies receipt in Boston with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If After-school supplies receipt is still submitted, change only After-school supplies receipt from submitted to needs-receipt. Leave North Loop mileage claim unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify expense review desk with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Report only returned fields, and label any missing requested detail as unknown. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: After-school supplies receipt was updated to needs-receipt after status verification.
